skill.name: report-generator
skill.description: Generate Markdown and HTML reports from datasets, metrics, and analysis notes with tables, charts, summaries, and recommendations.
skill.summary: Create structured Markdown and HTML reports with data summaries, charts, tables, and reusable templates.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: AI review found no malicious intent or prompt injection in SKILL.md. The shell execution, weak cryptography, sensitive-file, and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown fences, normal report text, CSS properties, and dictionary key handling. The only confirmed issue is a low-risk external Chart.js CDN dependency in generated HTML output.

low_findings.title: External CDN Dependency in Generated HTML
low_findings.description: The HTML report example loads Chart.js from jsDelivr. This is a legitimate reporting feature, but generated reports may contact a third-party CDN when opened.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line directly embeds an external script URL. No credential collection or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed context.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CDN Dependency in Generated HTML
dangerous_patterns.description: The HTML report example loads Chart.js from jsDelivr. This is a legitimate reporting feature, but generated reports may contact a third-party CDN when opened.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line directly embeds an external script URL. No credential collection or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed context.

content.value_statement: Teams often need clear reports from raw metrics and datasets. This skill creates structured Markdown and HTML reports with summaries, tables, charts, and recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Build executive summary reports with highlights, metrics, and recommendations.
Create data analysis reports with quality checks and statistical summaries.
Generate performance comparison reports against a baseline.
Format report output as Markdown tables and styled HTML.
Include chart sections using Chart.js or Python visualization examples.
Use predefined report templates for executive, technical, and comparison formats.
limitations: The skill provides examples and generation patterns, not a packaged command-line tool.
PDF export depends on external Python libraries being available in the environment.
HTML chart output may load Chart.js from an external CDN unless changed.
Generated insights depend on the quality and completeness of the supplied data.

use_cases.title: Summarize Business Metrics
use_cases.description: Turn weekly metrics into an executive report with highlights, changes, and action items.

use_cases.title: Document Dataset Quality
use_cases.description: Create a data quality report that explains missing values, data types, statistics, and duplicate records.

use_cases.title: Compare Technical Performance
use_cases.description: Produce a baseline comparison report for latency, throughput, error rates, or other service metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Summary Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use report-generator to create a concise Markdown report from this data. Include a title, executive summary, key metrics, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Data Quality Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use report-generator to analyze this dataset. Report missing values, type issues, duplicate records, statistical summaries, and practical next steps.

prompt_templates.title: HTML Report with Charts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use report-generator to create a styled HTML report with tables and chart sections. Avoid external assets unless I approve them.

prompt_templates.title: Baseline Comparison Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use report-generator to compare baseline and current metrics. Highlight regressions, improvements, thresholds, trend direction, and prioritized recommendations.

output_examples.input: Monthly sales metrics by region with current and previous period values.
output_examples.output: A Markdown executive report with regional highlights, change percentages, a performance table, and three recommended actions.

output_examples.input: Customer dataset with missing values, duplicate rows, and mixed column types.
output_examples.output: A data quality report that summarizes completeness, lists affected columns, explains duplicate impact, and suggests cleanup steps.

output_examples.input: Service latency, throughput, and error-rate metrics compared with a baseline.
output_examples.output: A performance report showing improved and regressed metrics, threshold status, charts, and remediation priorities.

best_practices: Provide clean source data, column meanings, and the intended audience before generating the report.
Ask for Markdown first when reviewing content, then request HTML or PDF formatting.
Review any external chart scripts before publishing HTML reports outside a trusted environment.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated recommendations as final decisions without domain review.
Do not publish reports with sensitive source data unless the content is redacted.
Do not rely on external CDN scripts in restricted or offline environments.

faq.question: Can this skill create both Markdown and HTML reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for Markdown reports, styled HTML reports, tables, and chart sections.

faq.question: Does it analyze raw datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize rows, columns, missing values, data types, duplicates, and numeric statistics.

faq.question: Can it generate charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for Chart.js, matplotlib, and seaborn based chart output.

faq.question: Does it export PDF files automatically?
faq.answer: It provides a PDF export pattern, but the environment needs the required PDF libraries installed.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: Only the HTML Chart.js example uses an external CDN. Users can replace it with a local asset.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
